label: Multi-Query Expand
description: Expand one question into N retrieval query variants to widen RAG recall (the 'multi-query retriever' pattern). The default path is a DETERMINISTIC rule-based expander — it derives reformulations (keyword-only, question→statement, synonym/paraphrase prefixes, and the original) without a model round-trip, so it is reproducible and harness-friendly. Wire it at the TOP of a RAG flow so each variant can be embedded + searched and the union of hits reranked. A single phrasing often misses chunks that a paraphrase would catch; expanding the query before retrieval raises recall.

settings.name: question
settings.label: Question
settings.description: The user's question to expand into multiple retrieval queries.

settings.name: numQueries
settings.label: Number of queries
settings.description: How many query variants to produce (including the original). The deterministic expander caps at the number of distinct reformulations it can derive.

settings.name: includeOriginal
settings.label: Include original query
settings.description: When true (default), the original question is always the first variant so retrieval never loses the user's exact phrasing.

ai.shortDescription: Expand one question into N retrieval query variants (deterministic, no model call by default) to widen RAG recall.
ai.whenToUse: At the top of a RAG flow, before embedding / knowledge_search. Each variant is embedded + searched and the union reranked, so a single phrasing doesn't miss relevant chunks.
ai.examplePrompt: expand the user's question into 4 retrieval queries
ai.promptHints: Set `question` to the user's question (templated). Reference the variants via {{steps.<id>.result.queries}} (a string[]); fan each into a knowledge_search or join them for a single retrieval call.

outputAssertions.name: multi_query_result_shape
outputAssertions.expression: result && typeof result.original === 'string' && Array.isArray(result.queries) && result.queries.length > 0 && typeof result.count === 'number'
outputAssertions.errorMessage: multi_query result must be { original, queries[] (non-empty), count, method }.
